title: Qila Mubarak
tagline: Qila Mubarak — heritage city of Punjab

overview.short: Qila Mubarak, also known as Gobindgarh or Bathinda Fort, is a historical monument in the heart of the city of Bathinda in Punjab, India.
overview.description: Qila Mubarak, also known as Gobindgarh or Bathinda Fort, is a historical monument in the heart of the city of Bathinda in Punjab, India. It is recognised as monument of national importance and maintained by Archaeological Survey of India. It has been in existence from 1100 to 1200 AD in its current place and is the oldest surviving fort in India. It was here that Razia Sultan, the first woman to take charge of the Delhi throne was incarcerated upon her defeat and dethroned.

topPlaces.name: Bathinda
topPlaces.description: Bathinda (Punjabi pronunciation: [bəʈʰɪ˩˥ɳɖä]) is a city and municipal corporation in Punjab, India. The city is the administrative headquarters of Bathinda district.

topPlaces.name: Guru Nanak Dev Thermal Plant
topPlaces.description: The Guru Nanak Dev Thermal Plant at Bathinda was one of the three coal-fired thermal power stations in Punjab (the other being at Lehra Mohabat and Ropar). It was a medium-sized power station with four units that were begun to be built in the early 1970s and completed in 1982.

topPlaces.name: Gill Patti
topPlaces.description: Gill Patti is a village in the tehsil and district Bathinda of Punjab, India.

items.note: Bathinda (Punjabi pronunciation: [bəʈʰɪ˩˥ɳɖä]) is a city and municipal corporation in Punjab, India. The city is the administrative headquarters of Bathinda district.

The Guru Nanak Dev Thermal Plant at Bathinda was one of the three coal-fired thermal power stations in Punjab (the other being at Lehra Mohabat and Ropar). It was a medium-sized power station with four units that were begun to be built in the early 1970s and completed in 1982.
Gill Patti is a village in the tehsil and district Bathinda of Punjab, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Qila Mubarak?
faq.a: The best time to visit Qila Mubarak is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Qila Mubarak?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport, Amritsar (168 km) and the nearest railway station is Ludhiana Junction (117 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Qila Mubarak?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Qila Mubarak at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Qila Mubarak?
faq.a: Stays in Qila Mubarak start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Qila Mubarak?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bathinda, Guru Nanak Dev Thermal Plant, Gill Patti.
